description: Figure 11 presents a flow chart illustrating the algorithmic implementation of the VUMAT user material subroutine developed for ABAQUS to simulate warm multiaxial forging (MAF) of Fe-30Mn-9Al-0.8C low-density steel using a modified Johnson-Cook constitutive model. The chart traces the subroutine execution from initialization and material constant computation through each increment step. At the beginning of a step, the algorithm checks whether the step time equals zero: if yes, it calculates trial stress assuming pure elastic behavior and terminates; if no, it proceeds to calculate the deviatoric trial stress difference (sigdif). The subsequent logic branches on whether sigdif exceeds zero—yielding updates the stress via the constitutive relation, while non-yielding sets equivalent plastic strain to zero—before both paths converge to update stress and state-dependent variables (SDVs), and finally update internal and dissipated inelastic energy. The caption states that the implemented subroutine employs six SDVs to incorporate the modified J-C equation, enabling finite element modeling of dislocation strengthening and grain refinement during the MAF process.
